title: Insecure functions should be avoided
description: An attacker typically provides input that exceeds the expected size. This could be through a text field in a user interface, a file that the program reads, or data sent over a network. The insecure function processes this input and places the result into a provided buffer.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Avoid using insecure functions. Use safer alternatives like strncpy, snprintf, or std::string.
